skill.name: to-issues
skill.description: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently grabbable issue tracker tickets using tracer-bullet vertical slices.
skill.summary: Convert engineering plans into approved, dependency-aware issue tracker tickets.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives: SKILL.md only mentions a slash command name and an issue label in prose. The weak cryptography findings are also false positives caused by ordinary Markdown text, with no algorithm use, code execution, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Slash Command Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:10 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line is prose that names `/setup-matt-pocock-skills`; it does not execute a shell command or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains a slash command reference in plain Markdown text. No executable shell syntax, script block, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Label Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:54 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The backticks format the issue tracker label `needs-triage` in Markdown and do not create code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown inline-code formatting around a label name. There is no shell context, subprocess call, or user-controlled command string.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:20, SKILL.md:26, SKILL.md:38, and SKILL.md:65, including a duplicate report for line 65. These lines contain natural-language instructions and Markdown labels, not cryptographic APIs or algorithm choices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose describing issue planning behavior. I found no cryptographic code, weak algorithm name used as an implementation choice, or security-sensitive transformation.

content.user_title: Convert Plans Into Trackable Engineering Issues
content.value_statement: Large plans are hard to turn into work that teams can assign and verify. This skill breaks plans into approved vertical slices and publishes issue tracker tickets.
actual_capabilities: Reads an existing plan, spec, PRD, issue, URL, or path from the conversation.
Fetches issue tracker context when the user provides an issue reference.
Breaks work into thin vertical slices that cover schema, API, UI, and tests when relevant.
Marks each proposed slice as HITL or AFK based on required human involvement.
Asks for user approval before publishing issues to the tracker.
Creates approved issues in dependency order with a consistent body template.
limitations: Requires issue tracker access and label vocabulary to be configured before publishing.
Depends on the quality and completeness of the plan or issue provided by the user.
Does not close, edit, or otherwise modify the parent issue.
May need user judgment for architecture, design, or dependency decisions.

use_cases.title: Turn a PRD into implementation tickets
use_cases.description: Convert a product requirement document into small issue tracker tickets with acceptance criteria and dependencies.

use_cases.title: Prepare a team-ready backlog
use_cases.description: Split a technical plan into independently grabbable slices that engineers can pick up without extra planning meetings.

use_cases.title: Break down an existing issue
use_cases.description: Read a parent issue and create child issues that preserve context while keeping each slice independently verifiable.

prompt_templates.title: Break down a short plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this plan to propose issue tracker tickets. Ask for approval before publishing: [paste plan].

prompt_templates.title: Convert a PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into vertical-slice issues with acceptance criteria, dependencies, and HITL or AFK labels: [paste PRD].

prompt_templates.title: Use an existing parent issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Read issue [issue reference], include relevant comments, and propose child issues. Do not publish until I approve the breakdown.

prompt_templates.title: Publish approved dependency order
prompt_templates.prompt: After I approve the proposed breakdown, publish the issues in dependency order and apply the needs-triage label.

output_examples.input: A checkout redesign PRD with shipping address, payment review, and confirmation requirements.
output: Issue: Capture shipping address from checkout form.
Issue: Review payment method before order submission.
Issue: Show order confirmation after successful payment.

output_examples.input: A parent issue requesting audit logging across the admin product.
output: AFK slice for recording one admin action end to end.
HITL slice for deciding retention policy.
AFK slice for displaying audit events in the admin UI.

output_examples.input: A migration plan that depends on schema changes and UI updates.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes blockers first, asks whether the dependency graph is correct, then publishes only after approval.

best_practices: Provide the source plan, parent issue, and expected issue tracker project before starting.
Review the proposed slice granularity and dependency order before approving publication.
Use project vocabulary and acceptance criteria that match the repository and team process.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to publish issues before reviewing the proposed breakdown.
Do not use vague plans that lack user outcomes or acceptance signals.
Do not use horizontal slices that isolate only database, API, or UI work.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates issue tracker tickets from a plan, spec, PRD, or existing issue after user approval.

faq.question: What is a vertical slice?
faq.answer: A vertical slice is a small end-to-end path that can be built, tested, and demonstrated on its own.

faq.question: Does it publish issues automatically?
faq.answer: No. It first proposes a numbered breakdown and asks the user to approve or revise it.

faq.question: Can it use an existing issue as the parent?
faq.answer: Yes. If given an issue reference, it reads the issue body and comments for context.

faq.question: Does it modify the parent issue?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly says not to close or modify any parent issue.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
